recommendations: add a minimal smoke test suite and report pass/fail status (current test status is empty)
reduce MCP surface area by exposing only stable/public APIs instead of hundreds of internal classes/functions
introduce an allowlist/denylist for endpoint generation to avoid unsafe or low-value exports (e.g.
stress tests
internal helpers)
fix naming collisions and duplicates in endpoints (e.g.
repeated names like `column`
`dfree`
`print_debug`
`stem`)
add endpoint namespacing (`whoosh.searching.Searcher`-style) to improve discoverability and avoid clashes
migrate packaging to `pyproject.toml` with explicit build-system/dependencies and keep `setup.py` as thin shim if needed
add `requirements-dev.txt`/lockfile and CI matrix for supported Python versions
modernize CI from legacy `.travis.yml` to GitHub Actions with lint/type/test jobs
add type hints for MCP adapter/service boundaries and run mypy/pyright in CI
add structured logging and error mapping in MCP service for clearer runtime diagnostics
implement request validation/timeouts/resource limits for heavy operations (`make_index`
large searches
stress endpoints)
add benchmark gates for core hot paths (searching/sorting/writing) and track regressions
document supported/unsupported modules in `README_MCP.md` with examples for top workflows (index
query
highlight)
add security review for dynamic loading/reflection paths (`find_object`
plugin wiring) and restrict unsafe imports
create contract tests for generated endpoints to ensure signatures/docs stay synced with source AST scanning
split very large modules incrementally (`searching.py`
`fields.py`
`writing.py`) behind compatibility layers to improve maintainability
add deprecation policy and versioned MCP API to prevent breaking downstream clients
include fuzz/property tests for parser-heavy components (`qparser`
date parsing
automata)
and add performance-safe defaults (pagination
result limits
cancellation) for search-related endpoints

success_factors: Workflow completed end-to-end with status=success across all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-based adapter mode matched repository profile (library-first Python package, high import feasibility 0.94, low intrusiveness risk).
Repository ingestion succeeded via zip fallback despite DeepWiki failure, allowing AST/module discovery to proceed.
MCP plugin started healthy over stdio, indicating baseline runtime viability of generated service scaffold.
failure_reasons: Quality gaps: original project test result is effectively unknown/empty despite a reported field value; no validated compatibility baseline.
DeepWiki analysis failed, reducing external semantic context and increasing reliance on static local scan.
Generated endpoint surface appears over-broad and collision-prone, which is a functional/design risk rather than a pipeline crash.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully as part of a 160.76s total run; zip fallback worked and imported ~167 files. No per-node timing provided.
node_performance.analysis_time: Successful static analysis and AST extraction over medium-complexity codebase; discovered extensive symbols/endpoints. No per-node timing provided.
node_performance.generation_time: Generation succeeded and produced expected MCP files and startup entrypoint, but metadata reports 0 LOC/0 size (instrumentation inconsistency).
node_performance.test_time: MCP service health check passed; original project tests were not meaningfully executed/reported, so confidence in behavioral correctness is limited.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Undetermined due to missing metrics (reported as 0). Pipeline likely lightweight-medium, but no empirical evidence.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Undetermined due to missing metrics (reported as 0).
resource_usage.disk_usage: Low-to-moderate expected; generated artifact set is small, but reported generated size=0 indicates telemetry/reporting gap.
